topic_name: Types of Computers Input Output Devices
subject: computer-science
description: Types of Computers Input Output Devices MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: How many basic types of computers are there based on operation?
mcqs.explanation: There are three basic types of computers by operation: Analog, Digital and Hybrid.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a type of computer based on operation?
mcqs.explanation: By operation, computers are classified as Analog, Digital and Hybrid. Quantum is a separate emerging category, not part of this classic 3-way classification.

mcqs.q: A digital computer is based on which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Digital computers operate on counting using a binary number system with only two digits: 0 and 1.

mcqs.q: An analog computer is based on which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Analog computers work on a continuous range of values — continuous measurement.

mcqs.q: A hybrid computer is based on:
mcqs.explanation: Hybrid computers combine both counting (digital) and measurement (analog) features.

mcqs.q: General-purpose PCs are an example of which type of computer?
mcqs.explanation: General-purpose Personal Computers (PCs) are digital computers — they operate on 0/1 binary signals.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an example of an analog computer?
mcqs.explanation: Speedometers, ECG machines and weather-forecasting devices are classic analog computers based on continuous measurement.

mcqs.q: Petrol pumps, modems and jet plane simulators are examples of which type of computer?
mcqs.explanation: Petrol pumps, modems, simulators and jet plane systems are hybrid computers — they combine analog measurement with digital counting.

mcqs.q: Which number system does a digital computer use?
mcqs.explanation: Digital computers use the binary number system with only two digits: 0 and 1.

mcqs.q: Which type of computer is mostly used to automate complicated physical processes and machines?
mcqs.explanation: Hybrid computers are mostly used with automatic operations of complicated physical processes and machines.

mcqs.q: An ECG (Electrocardiogram) machine is an example of which type of computer?
mcqs.explanation: ECG machines work on continuous measurement of electrical signals — they are analog computers.

mcqs.q: Which type of computer typically gives output in graph or picture form?
mcqs.explanation: Analog computers typically produce output as graphs or pictures because they operate on continuous measurement.

mcqs.q: A Personal Computer (PC) typically supports how many users at a time?
mcqs.explanation: A PC is a single-user computer with a moderately powerful microprocessor.

mcqs.q: Which computer has a more powerful microprocessor than a PC but is still single-user?
mcqs.explanation: A Workstation is a single-user computer with a more powerful microprocessor than a typical PC.

mcqs.q: A Mini Computer is capable of supporting:
mcqs.explanation: A Mini Computer is a multi-user system capable of supporting hundreds of users simultaneously.

mcqs.q: Which type of computer is mainly used in banks?
mcqs.explanation: Mainframe computers are mainly used in banks. They support hundreds of users simultaneously.

mcqs.q: Which is the fastest type of computer that can execute hundreds of millions of instructions per second?
mcqs.explanation: Supercomputers are extremely fast — they can execute hundreds of millions of instructions per second.

mcqs.q: NASA's computers used for space exploration are examples of:
mcqs.explanation: NASA uses supercomputers for tasks like space exploration, global weather forecasting and complex engineering simulations.

mcqs.q: Supercomputers are used for which of the following tasks?
options: Global weather forecasting and space exploration
mcqs.explanation: Supercomputers are used for global weather forecasting, graphic image processing, engineering design and space exploration.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the smallest in terms of size and power?
mcqs.explanation: A Personal Computer is the smallest of the listed types in terms of size and processing power.

mcqs.q: Which two types of computers are typically multi-user systems?
mcqs.explanation: Mini Computers and Mainframe Computers are multi-user systems supporting hundreds of users simultaneously.

mcqs.q: A computer that has a moderately powerful microprocessor and is designed for a single user is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A Personal Computer (PC) has a moderately powerful microprocessor and is designed for a single user.

mcqs.q: Which is the most common and popular input device?
mcqs.explanation: The keyboard is the most common and popular input device — it encodes characters by the depression of keys.

mcqs.q: The standard key arrangement on a computer keyboard is called:
mcqs.explanation: The standard key arrangement on a keyboard is called QWERTY, named after the first six letters of the top row.

mcqs.q: Which is the most popular pointing device?
mcqs.explanation: The mouse is the most popular pointing device used with computers.

mcqs.q: Which input device transmits X-Y hand coordinates to position a cursor on the screen?
mcqs.explanation: A joystick transmits X-Y hand coordinates to the computer to position a cursor on the screen, similar to a mouse.

mcqs.q: A light-sensitive pointing input device used to select or modify text/data on a screen is called:
mcqs.explanation: A Light Pen is a light-sensitive pointing input device commonly used to select or modify text or data on a screen.

mcqs.q: Which input device is mostly used in notebook/laptop computers instead of a mouse?
mcqs.explanation: The Track Ball is an input device mostly used in notebook/laptop computers in place of a mouse.

mcqs.q: Which input device works more like a photocopy machine?
mcqs.explanation: A Scanner works more like a photocopy machine — it captures images and text from physical documents.

mcqs.q: Which input device is used to change information into digital form?
mcqs.explanation: A Digitizer is a device that changes information into digital form.

mcqs.q: MICR stands for:
mcqs.explanation: MICR stands for Magnetic Ink Card Reader. It is generally used in banks for processing cheques.

mcqs.q: MICR (Magnetic Ink Card Reader) is generally used in:
mcqs.explanation: MICR is generally used in banks because of the large number of cheques to be processed every day.

mcqs.q: OCR stands for:
mcqs.explanation: OCR stands for Optical Character Reader — an input device used to read printed text.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is used to read printed text?
mcqs.explanation: OCR (Optical Character Reader) is the input device used to read printed text and convert it into editable digital form.

mcqs.q: OMR stands for:
mcqs.explanation: OMR stands for Optical Mark Reader — a special type of optical scanner used to recognise marks made by pen or pencil (e.g. on MCQ sheets).

mcqs.q: Which device is most commonly used to read MCQ-style answer sheets?
mcqs.explanation: OMR (Optical Mark Reader) is used to read MCQ-style answer sheets, recognising the type of mark made by pen or pencil.

mcqs.q: A device used for reading bar-coded data is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A Bar Code Reader is a device used for reading bar-coded data — typically used in shops and warehouses.

mcqs.q: Which input device is used to input sound that is then stored in digital form?
mcqs.explanation: A Microphone is an input device used to input sound, which is then stored in a digital form by the computer.

mcqs.q: A Touch Screen is a:
options: Input device that accepts touch directly on the screen
mcqs.explanation: A Touch Screen is an input device that accepts touch input directly on the screen surface.

mcqs.q: A Web Camera is a device that:
options: Captures and transmits video/image input to the computer
mcqs.explanation: A Web Camera (webcam) captures and transmits video or image input to the computer.

mcqs.q: What is the formal name for the computer monitor?
mcqs.explanation: A monitor is formally called a VDU — Visual Display Unit. It is the main output device of a computer.

mcqs.q: VDU stands for:
mcqs.explanation: VDU stands for Visual Display Unit — the formal name for a computer monitor.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the main output device of a computer?
mcqs.explanation: The Monitor (VDU) is the main output device of a computer — it visually displays processed information.

mcqs.q: A flat-panel display has reduced volume, weight and power requirement compared to:
mcqs.explanation: Flat-panel displays have reduced volume, weight and power requirement compared to traditional CRT monitors.

mcqs.q: Which printer utilises a laser beam to produce an image on a drum?
mcqs.explanation: A Laser Printer utilises a laser beam to produce an image on a drum, which is then transferred to paper using toner.

mcqs.q: An impact printer operates by:
options: Striking a metal or plastic head against an ink ribbon
mcqs.explanation: An Impact Printer operates by striking a metal or plastic head against an ink ribbon — much like an old typewriter.

mcqs.q: A device that prints one character at a time is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A Character Printer is a device that prints one character at a time.

mcqs.q: A Dot Matrix printer prints characters as:
mcqs.explanation: A Dot Matrix Printer prints characters as a combination of dots, formed by an array of pins striking an ink ribbon.

mcqs.q: Which printer is a non-impact character printer that prints by spraying patterns of ink on paper?
mcqs.explanation: An Inkjet Printer is a non-impact character printer that prints by spraying patterns of ink on paper from a nozzle or jet.

mcqs.q: The most recent development in printing — which creates three-dimensional objects from a CAD model — is the:
mcqs.explanation: The 3D Printer is the most recent development in printing — it creates a three-dimensional object from a CAD (Computer-Aided Design) model.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an audio output device?
mcqs.explanation: A Speaker is an audio output device that converts digital signals into sound.

mcqs.q: CD and DVD are examples of:
mcqs.explanation: CD and DVD are optical media — they are commonly classified as output/storage devices.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a STORAGE device?
mcqs.explanation: Flash Memory is a storage device. Other examples include Optical Disk, Local Drives, Magnetic Tape and Magnetic Disk.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a PROCESSING device?
mcqs.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is a processing device. Other processing devices include Motherboard, Processor, ROM and SMPS.

mcqs.q: RAM stands for:
mcqs.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory — a processing/primary memory device.

mcqs.q: ROM stands for:
mcqs.explanation: ROM stands for Read Only Memory — a non-volatile memory used as a processing device.

mcqs.q: SMPS in a computer stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SMPS stands for Switched Mode Power Supply — a key processing-side component that supplies regulated power to a computer.
